Reeking Skunk  

Monster
  • View
  • History
  • Media
  • Discuss
  • Tools
    • Main page
    • Print this page
    • What links here
    • Recent changes
    • Wiki search

Reupload - September 10th, 2009

Uploaded March 14th, 2026 by iventheassassin

  • Placeholder is: a skunk
  • This mob spawns at /waypoint 000 (roams) in Toxxulia Forest.
  • Faction: None
  • Attackable: Yes
  • Findable (via Ctrl-F): No
  • Aggros even when grey: No

  • Maximum hit: 136
  • Rootable/snareable: No

Non-KOS; runs a bit slower than SOW speed.

Categories: Templatized | EverQuest
This page last modified 2026-03-14 08:57:11.

Level: 45
Expansion:
NPC Added: 0000-00-00 00:00:00
NPC Last Updated: 2025-12-24 08:03:54
Known Loot:
Collector's Skunk Scent Gland
Scent Gland
Skunk Eye
Skunk Scent Gland
Skunk Tail

Known Habitats:
  Toxxulia Forest
Quests:
  Berserker Epic: Kerasian Axe of Ire

Report a correction

« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Not in the "Original" content
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2024 at 3:44 AM Rating: Decent
Mr Sexy Pants
4 posts
This is incorrectly marked as Original for the expansion. Oakwynd is currently on Planes of Power and I can confirm he does not spawn yet. The PHs for him don't spawn, just the regular trash mobs zone-wide and other skunks don't seem to be guaranteed spawns from any of them. Tracked with a ranger while I hunted down every skunk in the zone for 30 minutes.
▲ Page top
Not in the "Original" content
QuoteReply
# Dec 23 2025 at 11:37 AM Rating: Good
shotznow
Scholar
24 posts
Should be Gates of Discord as that is when zerkers release
▲ Page top
Zerk 1.0
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2022 at 10:39 AM Rating: Decent
Defresco
7 posts
Killed every single thing I saw in the area just south of Erudin, with a focus on skunks.
Macro hidecorpse all and /tar skunk if you don't have a tracker and just run around. Took me between 20-30 mins, wasn't really timing it. Got about 8 pieces of defiant gear.
▲ Page top
Spawn info
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2014 at 2:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1715559370129
View User Forum
169 posts
As near as I could tell from watching track while looking for this skunk, there are 2 "a skunk" that continuously spawn every 15 to 30 seconds. After you kill the last skunk, watch track. Another "a skunk" will appear about 10 to 20 seconds later. If you don't go kill that one, another will pop in about 30 seconds. The only time I saw this NOT occur, was when the Reeking popped instead of the "a skunk". I killed every " a skunk" on track until I got these 2 up, then just kept killing them until the Reeking finally spawned. Was way easier to get this guy than the Gorilla or the Kobold. Each of them took just over 2 hours each. This one, I got 2 Reeking Skunks in about 30 minutes.
▲ Page top
newbie area mob increase
QuoteReply
# May 07 2014 at 12:56 AM Rating: Decent
subzero42
Scholar
26 posts
Now with the increase in mobs in the newbie zones this is a real pain to kill all the skunks
▲ Page top
8 Second spawn!
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2013 at 12:35 PM Rating: Default
Glorfendill
View User Forum
48 posts
On the north side of the river in Toxx kill any/all skunks, when you find "a skunk" that respawns in about 8 seconds you've found the PH, I saw "a large skunk" once shortly before I locked in on the 8 second spawn so he is probably part of the cycle a skunk/a large skunk/Reeking Skunk!

Enjoy
____________________________
Glorf
Future Monk
Faceless Uprising, Selo
▲ Page top
not just a_large_skunk
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2011 at 11:42 PM Rating: Decent
fronglo
Scholar
4,445 posts
I was able to get this thing to spawn and I am positive there was no a_large_skunk just a_skunk. He spawned towards the top of the map. took about 15 minutes of druid tracking and killing all a_skunk. Never even saw a_large_skunk.
____________________________
Hi
▲ Page top
2010 Update
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2010 at 4:27 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1708906582172
Scholar
31 posts
I followed Luthair's post for this, basically:

Run to the northern section of Toxx and stay north of the river (the Erudin newbie area) and kill all (and only) "a skunk" on track.

After awhile only 1 "a skunk" will be up, which once killed will respawn every 15-20 seconds or so at different locations, I found it at the following:

228.44, -782.83, 84.21
1125.97, -376.60, 100.30
357.01, -87.39, 59.78
1060.32, -153.46, 92.83
1483.46, -801.77, 73.36
1139.59, -976.43, 111.94

Finally, after about an hour camping the 15 second "a skunk", the reeking spawned, which I found wandering at:
1356.12, -406.19, 90.92

I did try slaughtering everything around the PoK book and the druid rings for awhile but Luthair's approach appeared to work, unless the NPC guards killed the other PH for me.

Edited, Dec 17th 2010 5:49pm by Tetras
▲ Page top
Skunk
QuoteReply
# Feb 15 2009 at 12:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591964078263
5 posts
Got him to spawn after killing all over the place saw him spawn @ 972 -604 120 on the spot I had killed a previous skunk because the corpse was still there.

a skunk
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2008 at 7:17 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593972017881
View User Forum
Sage
1,524 posts
Killed nothing but "a skunk" for about 10 min and got Reeking Skunk to spawn in the central part of the zone.
▲ Page top
Reeking Skunk
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2008 at 8:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966786975
10 posts
Im just gonna take a stab in the dark here but it seems Reeking Skunk is most likely thrown in with the newby mobs spawn cycle. Since ive seen the numbers of skunks increase as I killed trash mobs I'm gonna guess that Reeking Skunk can spawn in place of any noob yard mobs at any of those spawn points. Not a certain spawn point. Best advice, do what Im doing... load up a buncha bards and park them on mob spawn points playing AE songs while you run around killing everything you see.
▲ Page top
Spawning
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2007 at 10:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592790120983
Scholar
65 posts
If you happen to be the only person in the zone, using a tracker only kill 'a skunk' (or 'a large skunk' if you see one, I didn't), they share a table with other animals so shortly only one will be up. The PH is (in my experience) always 'a skunk' with a respawn of 15-20 seconds.
▲ Page top
Spawning
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2008 at 12:15 PM Rating: Decent
Krugessin
10 posts
I agree with this post. Tried some different approaches with no luck and ended up plainly killing all skunks on track. No one else was killing anything in zone, and after a while there was just one "a skunk" that kept re-popping quickly when killed.

# When "Reeking Skunk" spawned, "a skunk" stopped appearing.

# When Reeking Skunk was killed, "a skunk" started doing its merry re-pop dance again.


Never saw "a large skunk" or similar on track, but I guess it might be part of the spawn table too.

After zerker got his drop, I went on to kill maybe 5-10 more of the lonely "a skunk", and within maybe 15 min had another Reeking up.


/Krugessin The Normal Height Wizard
▲ Page top
Agree
QuoteReply
# Oct 06 2007 at 9:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772978201
Scholar
62 posts
Ok I have to agree, I stopped killing skunks and killed everything but skunks, and with in 45 minutes I had the Reeking Skunk Pop, never saw a large skunk. This was after 3 different sessions of an hour+ of killing skunks. I just worked the North East area around PoK book to Eurdine, the Reeking seem to spawn South of the Eurdin zone in. I had my ranger just bow mobs and I went target kill target kill, everything but skunks. Seem to be the best way to get this thing to pop. Hope this helps.

▲ Page top
Spawn Tactics!
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2007 at 3:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592294048169
8 posts
I made these observations while attempting to spawn the Reeking Skunk.

First: I hunted down regular skunks. No luck. Time to change gears.

Second: I killed mobs all around the entrance to Erudin and the book to the Plane of Knowledge, as well as over by Sentinel Creot and the Druid rings. My goal was to spawn MORE SKUNKS. So, I left the regular a skunk alive and continued to run around killing the mobs.

Third: On Bard track I noticed a large skunk had spawned. I knew this had to be important. So I killed it. (That's a natural and healthy reaction.) It immediately respawned another large skunk. Killed that. POP!!!

I used the Bard song "High Sun" to find the location for that particular spawn @ 826.29, -885.56

Final Observations:Leaving the skunks up allowed me to move on from the "incorrect" NPCs and onto the actual place holder(s). I hope any of this information is found to be useful. :)
▲ Page top
My spawning luck
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2006 at 4:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592288262087
54 posts
Ok, well from what I can tell, killing skunks PROBABLY spawns him, but the first time i killed him, he spawned a skelly, second time, a spiderling. I dunno. Skunks are random from anything, and they must be in the zone; he must spawn from a skunk... i guess
▲ Page top
solo
QuoteReply
# Aug 26 2006 at 1:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592772917456
153 posts
Easy to solo him, really, just keep in mind that he is immune to changes in his run speed.

My 50 druid soloed him easy, with Drifting Death, Drones of Doom and a few Starfires. Had a few k hp, 750-pt nuke brought him down about 15%. Saw him near the ent to Erudin. She just kited it with sow, was pretty easy, but when it hit her, it hit fairly hard. She had KEI and Virt on. Piece of cheesecake.
▲ Page top
not aggro
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2006 at 2:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758325296
Scholar
47 posts
/cons indif to my agnostic erudite chanter

47 pally
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2005 at 3:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591825277241
16 posts
i was running from erudin to paineel and ran into this guy, conned indeffrent and dk blue... so i was forced to kill him, 47 pally, with virt and self brells i had 4.7k hp, also had kei but didn't need it. i even forgot to give myself haste before staring but i killed him fine without. i used lay hands but proably could have gotten away without. ended fight with 45%hp and 80%mana. was immune to stun and root.

Just did him for my baby Berserker's epic with a 53 Bard. Low HP, doesn't summon, very easy mob.

Tough Little Butt-Muncher
QuoteReply
# May 27 2005 at 11:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591837817090
7 posts
He conned DB to my lvl 46 Pally, I was pretty well buffed and was soloing.....or was I?? I think he was soloing me....was immune to Holy Might. He took me down to 18% and he was still at about 80%. I used my LOH...and he took me down again to 38% before I booked it outta there. Was hitting me with dual attacks of 85, 65...and a few 113's.
▲ Page top
Skunk Dead
QuoteReply
# May 15 2005 at 7:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770826864
View User Forum
26 posts
Found between Emil and druid ring. Conned DB to my 56 Ogre Berserker and indifferent. I had just lost virtue and had 3700 hp and 1100 AC. I used Edge of Envelopement and attacked. Stunned with Head Strike and Frenzied. I had 29 percent health when skunk died. I used my hit every time disc and Battle Cry. Harder than Gnashing Kobold but no problem. Dropped my epic piece Scent Gland. Total time in Toxx was about 10 minutes. Fight about 2 minutes.

Edited, Sun May 15 20:23:56 2005

QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2004 at 4:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591823381017
16 posts
It conned indifferent to both an ogre warrior and a wood elf druid so I imagine its simply non-kos to all. Took it down with the afformentioned pair when we were both level 33, just to see if we could. You can't snare it(you get an immune message) but its easy enough to aggro kite around with SoW.

▲ Page top
Once upon a run through Tox...
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2004 at 3:43 AM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
35 posts
Conned dubious to my 52 barb warrior, was hittin too hard so i jokingly shouted "train to erudin" even though he (**the skunk) is not kos to players. a 65 chanter happened to be there, and of course most people go stark raving mad when they see a named mob, so i zoned in and out of erudin to clear aggro, and there's the skunk wailing on the chanter while she was desperately trying to nuke it to death, most likely drooling in jittery anticipation at the phat lootz she thought she was going to nab. the mob and the enc's health stayed about equal, and i think the chanter actually won with 4% health. I briefly considered helping but figured the bloodthirsty freak would petition and spread bad word about me, so i just sat and watched lol i wish i could have witnessed IRL her reaction to the spoils of her victory =)

**edit

Edited, Wed Jul 28 04:45:16 2004
▲ Page top
Reeking Skunk
QuoteReply
# Jul 04 2004 at 12:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261117371
5 posts
Hello - This mob IMHO does not belong here. However I have have tracking info for those wanting to find him for the Beserker Epic...

He travels from Emil (Merchant near Eru entrance - seen him just south of here many times) to PoK book or Tran (Merchant) - I have also seen him around the Druid Circle slightly north. He normally can be found within those borders on the map. I soloed him at lvl 52 as wiz, he conned dark blue. It took my best spell (ice comet) 5 casts at 1100 - 1300 to get him down to 4% hp. As said before in other posts, he resists snare and slow spells. He hits quickly with 4-5 attacks and sometimes hits for 136+ per pop. I did not know he was epic at the time or would have offered the scent gland to someone else. GL with this mob and beware newbies near Eru zone.

Edited, Sun Jul 4 14:05:20 2004
▲ Page top
ROUS's
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2004 at 2:36 PM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
Logged in my 50 druid. And there he was standing by the merchant's hut. He was huge. For a moment I thought he was a ROUS (Rodent of unusual size), like when the wolves were all huge all over Norath. Nope, he was named. I was geared for 222 wisdom (with my sleeves). Hes not exactly an easy fight for a 50 druid, or maybe I play wrong? I was sowed and skinned. I first tried snare, silly me. Immune to snare and root. ok. Ran around like an idiot. What makes him paticularly nasty is he would get to me before any spells finnished. The only dot that landed before he got to me was immoliate. I had full mana before this. I dotted the hell out of him. The final sliver I had of mana for the last DoT, went instead to a very nervous SoW. At one point, I ran away to memorize 'terrorize animal'. I think I ran far enough to lose agro. He started gaining life fast. Tracked him down. dotted, and so on. By the end I had about 23% health and 2% mana. He was just very persistant. Chased me to the end, and perma sow'ed. He never slowed down. He is not an 'animal' I guess hes a freaking Monster!

I am still a bit new to what my druid can do at times. As long as its not a caster and my spells land, I am still amazed. I actually was teased by some when I exclaimed I had killed him? He was not full on easy. Id assume resistant to slow from what I read here, and maybe fear? He resisted my 'lightning blast' also.

I too felt bad learning he was for Zerker epic, after looting the gland.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.
